tools.name: process_data
tools.description: Processes data from the input source and transforms it according to the specified format. This tool supports multiple data formats including JSON, XML, CSV, YAML, TOML, and many others. It can handle nested data structures, arrays, maps, and complex object hierarchies. The processing pipeline includes validation, transformation, normalization, and output formatting stages. Each stage can be configured independently through the parameters. The validation stage checks for schema compliance, data type correctness, range constraints, and referential integrity. The transformation stage applies user-defined mappings, filters, aggregations, and calculations. It supports mathematical operations, string manipulations, date parsing, and regular expression matching. The normalization stage handles character encoding, whitespace normalization, case folding, and Unicode canonicalization. The output formatting stage renders the processed data in the desired format with configurable indentation, line endings, and encoding settings. Advanced features include streaming processing for large datasets, incremental updates for real-time data feeds, and batch processing for bulk operations. The tool also provides comprehensive logging, error reporting, and performance metrics. Error handling follows a configurable strategy that can be set to strict mode where any error aborts processing, lenient mode where errors are logged and processing continues, or custom mode where user-defined error handlers are invoked. Performance optimization includes lazy evaluation, memoization, parallel processing, and result caching. The tool integrates with external services for enrichment and validation through configurable connectors. Supported connectors include HTTP REST APIs, GraphQL endpoints, database connections via JDBC and ODBC, message queues like RabbitMQ and Kafka, and file system watchers. Security features include input sanitization, output encoding, access control through role-based permissions, and audit logging. The tool can operate in sandboxed mode where all file system and network access is restricted to a configurable whitelist. Configuration can be provided inline through parameters, loaded from configuration files in JSON or YAML format, or fetched from remote configuration services. The tool supports hot-reloading of configuration changes without requiring restart. Monitoring and observability are provided through OpenTelemetry-compatible metrics, distributed tracing, and structured logging. Health checks and readiness probes are available for container orchestration platforms. The tool can be extended through a plugin system that supports loading custom processors, validators, formatters, and connectors at runtime. Plugins are loaded from a configurable plugin directory and can be written in TypeScript or JavaScript. Each plugin must implement a standard interface and can declare dependencies on other plugins. The plugin lifecycle includes initialization, activation, deactivation, and cleanup phases. Plugins can register event handlers for processing pipeline events and can modify the processing behavior through interceptors and middleware patterns.
